candidates.why: Debt cost metric on the balance sheet, unrelated to cost of goods/services sold.

variables.notes: R:c. Candidate list clearly misses the correct concept. Selected 'Operating costs' from the full income statement preview because it is the main direct operating cost line immediately below revenue and above net operating income, which best matches cost of sales/service costs for this real-estate/service business. Manual review recommended because property companies may classify some direct costs differently. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Operating costs

candidates.why: Administrative-type operating cost, but likely tied to property operations rather than central overhead.
Component of lease/property administration costs; potentially partly overhead but may be property-operating.
Clear overhead/administrative bucket above operating profit.
Direct administrative labor component of overhead; appropriate SG&A component.
Administrative overhead component; appropriate SG&A component.
Explicit SG&A label exists, but appears mostly empty in the preview; per rules include it alongside a nearby overhead bucket/components for coverage.

variables.notes: Selected the explicit SG&A row plus administrative overhead components because the explicit SG&A line exists but is mostly empty in the preview. Excluded 'Central administrative costs' because component rows exist and its subtotal includes depreciation, which should be excluded. Excluded lease/property administration rows as they appear closer to property operating costs than central SG&A.
No dedicated research and development expense line appears in the provided income statement.
